MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION .
Off Calendar Oct-07-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT EPIC REAL ESTATE & ASSO. INC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact whether plaintiff signed the cited independent contractor agreement.

"The declarations in support of a motion for summary judgment [or adjudication] should be strictly construed, while the opposing declarations should be liberally construed." (Fernandez v. Alexander (2019) 31 Cal.App.5th 770, 779.)

The provisions of the Unemployment Insurance Code incorporated in Business and Professions Code section 10032(b) provide that a real estate agent is an independent contractor if three conditions are satisfied: (1) the agent is licensed; (2) the agent is paid through commissions; and (3) the agent has signed an independent contractor agreement. (See Whitlach v. Premier Valley (2022) 86 Cal. App. 5th 673, 690.)

In this case, defendant asserts that plaintiff signed an independent contractor agreement on February 25, 2019. (UMF 9.) Plaintiff, however, contests that he ever signed that agreement and the conflicting evidence creates a triable issue of material fact that the trier of fact must resolve. (Azari Decl., par. 18, Ex. 3; Khalatbari Decl., pars. 9-11.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-10-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS EPIC REAL ESTATE & ASSO. INC., LUZON PROJECT MGMT., LLC, MANZAR AZARI, JOHN P. EVANGELATOS AND EUGENIA MANTZOROS'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants' demurrer to the complaint is adjudged as follows.

The complaint pleads that plaintiff worked for defendants "approximately between February 25 to November 19, 2019." (Id. at 2:17-19.) Elsewhere plaintiff pleads that he was terminated on November 10, 2019. (Id. at 7:6.) Emergency Rule 9 tolled statutes of limitations from April 6 to October 1, 2020, 178 days. (Id. at 3:4-7.) The complaint was filed December 29, 2022.

Defendants argue that three-year statutes of limitations bar causes of action 1-6 "from the period February 25, 2019 to July 3, 2019." (Memo. 3:12-4:24.) It is unclear how defendants calculated this. In any event, defendants effectively concede that relief for several months of plaintiff's work history in July-November 2019 is not time-barred. A "general demurrer does not lie as to a portion of a cause of action and, if any part of a cause of action is properly pleaded, the demurrer will be overruled." (Elder v. Pacific Bell Telephone Co. (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 841, 856 n.14.) The demurrer is overruled as to counts 1-6.

Defendants argue that two-year statutes of limitations bar causes of action 9 and 10. (Memo. 5:1-14.) The facts pled by the complaint are too skimpy to tell. (Id. at 4:17-21, 17:1-28.) The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to counts 9 and 10.

Breaches of contract and of the good faith covenant are often pled in the alternative in the California courts. The demurrer is overruled as to count 11.

Defendants argue that a three-year statute of limitations bars cause of action 12, assuming the statute was triggered some time in "June 2019," though they also mention subsequent months. (Memo. 6:18-7:3.) Emergency Rule 9 tolling applies. The demurrer is overruled as to count 12.

The opposition does not appear to address the demurrer to count 13; it is sustained with leave to amend.

The same limitations analysis applies to count 14 as to count 12. However, a fiduciary duty is not adequately pled. The demurrer as to count 14 is sustained with leave to amend.

The same limitations analysis applies to count 16 as to count 12. The demurrer is overruled as to count 16.

Order signed. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (302/RBU)
